我已经把每件事都做对了，但是已经是凌晨2点了，我还是睡不着觉。我在睡觉前已经冥想了30分钟。为了保险起见，我甚至吃了几片褪黑激素药片。然而，在这里，我完全醒着，担心第二天上班会变得多么疲惫。
我的问题并不是独一无二的。在美国，每10个成年人中就有一个会经历慢性失眠1。只需一晚睡眠不佳，就会演变成长期的睡眠问题。这是因为大多数睡眠问题源于焦虑和担忧2.你越担心睡眠不足，你的睡眠就越差。
对我来说，我的睡眠问题从大学一开始就开始了。我刚开始一份软件工程师的工作，我开始感受到压力。我有几个重要的最后期限快到了，有一天晚上我发现自己辗转反侧，无法入睡。我的脑子一直在飞驰，一直想着我要做的所有工作，直到我终于睡着了。
我以为就是这样了--一个不寻常的夜晚，但后来又发生了。这一次，我的观点改变了。我开始担心睡眠不足会如何影响我的一天。我在网上查阅了基本的睡眠卫生提示，并决定额外早点上床睡觉，以确保我能弥补失去的睡眠。结果适得其反，我花了几个小时才最终入睡。
在这一点上，我的睡眠挣扎开始变得更加规律。我下载了几款冥想应用程序，希望冥想和聆听舒缓的声音能帮助我睡得更好。我甚至在入睡时间太长的晚上求助于柜台上的助眠器。结果好坏参半--我会更快入睡，但随着时间的推移，我会回到睡眠不佳的状态。
我最终屈服了，决定试试处方药。我读了很多关于它们的书，由于它们的长期副作用，我非常不情愿。在去看医生的时候，我问她是否可以给我开一些帮助我睡眠的药。她说她可以，但不会是药物治疗。取而代之的是，她推荐了CBT-I(失眠认知行为疗法)治疗师。
CBT-I是一种以证据为基础(在临床试验中得到证实)的技术，用于治疗睡眠问题3.有两种驱动力诱导睡眠-睡眠驱动力和精神唤醒。你的睡眠驱动力就是你上床睡觉时感觉有多困/有多累。你的精神唤醒是当你上床睡觉时你感觉到的快速思维和警觉性。当你的睡眠驱动力很高，而你的唤醒水平较低时，就更容易入睡。CBT-I规定了一个睡眠时间表，以确保你有一个高睡眠驱动力。它还教会你减少担忧和降低唤醒的技巧。
CBT-I对治疗睡眠问题非常有效。临床试验表明，80%遵循良好CBT-I计划的人在4号结束时会有正常的睡眠。这一结果也是永久性的，不像助眠剂只有在你服用它们的情况下才有效。
我和一位治疗师一起接受了为期3个月的CBT-I项目。我们开始限制我在床上的时间，以增加我的睡眠驱动力。我开始注意到，在我第一周的最后一周，我睡觉的时间减少了。到了第三周，我睡得更多了，持续入睡的时间变少了。节目结束时，我每晚的睡眠时间超过7个小时。更重要的是，我没有整天担心我的睡眠。
CBT-I并不是一个简单的解决方案。睡眠时间表很难遵循，结果也不是立竿见影的。这需要时间和精力，但永久改善睡眠的回报是非常值得的。我很感激有一位教练帮我完成了旅途。如果你决定试一试，让我知道它是否能帮助你改善睡眠。在这里，祝你早日睡得更好。CBT-我改变了我的生活，但这个项目花了我700美元。我很幸运，有钱买得起它，但对许多人来说，它远远超出了价格范围。我们建造了Sleepedy，希望让教练指导的CBT-I项目更容易负担得起。如果您想了解更多信息，请访问我们的网站或下载下面的iOS应用程序。

I'd done everything right, yet it was 2 am and I still couldn't sleep. I had meditated for 30 minutes before I went to bed. I even took a couple of melatonin pills right after to be sure. Yet, here I was wide awake dreading how exhausted I would be the next day at work.
 My problem wasn't unique. In America, one in every 10 adults will experience chronic insomnia  1. It takes just one poor night of sleep to devolve into long term sleeping problems. This is because the majority of sleeping problems stem from anxiety and worry  2.  The more you worry about not getting enough sleep, the worse your sleep gets.
 For me, my sleeping issues began right out of college. I had started a job as a software engineer and I was starting to feel the pressure. I had a couple of big deadlines coming up and I found myself tossing and turning unable to sleep one night. My mind kept racing, thinking about all the work I had until I finally fell asleep.
 I thought that was it — an abnormal night, but then it happened again. This time, my outlook changed. I started worrying about how not getting enough sleep would affect my day. I looked up basic sleep hygiene tips online and decided to go to bed extra early to make sure I made up for lost sleep. This ended up backfiring and it took a few hours before I could finally sleep.
 At this point, my sleeping struggles started becoming more regular. I downloaded a couple of meditation apps in the hopes that meditating and listening to soothing sounds would help me sleep better. I even resorted to over the counter sleeping aids on nights when it took me too long to fall asleep.  The results were mixed — I'd fall asleep faster but over time I'd revert back to having poor sleep.
 I finally caved and decided to try prescription medication. I had read a lot about them and was very reluctant because of their long term side effects. On a visit to my doctor, I asked her if she could prescribe me something to help me sleep. She said she said she could, but it wasn't going to be medication. Instead, she put in a referral to a CBT-I (Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia) therapist.
 CBT-I is an evidence-based (proven in clinical trials) technique used to treat sleep problems  3.  Two driving forces induce sleep — sleep drive & mental arousal. Your sleep drive is how sleepy/tired you feel when going to bed. Your mental arousal is the racing thoughts and alertness you feel when you go to bed. When your sleep drive is high and your arousal levels are low; it is easier to fall asleep. CBT-I prescribes a sleep schedule that ensures you have a high sleep drive. It also teaches you techniques to worry less and reduce your arousal.
 CBT-I is extremely effective in treating sleeping problems. Clinical trials have shown that 80% of people that follow a good CBT-I program will have normal sleep by the end  4. The results are also permanent, unlike sleeping aids which are effective only as long as you are taking them  5.
 I underwent a CBT-I program with a therapist for 3 months. We started by restricting the time I spent in bed to increase my sleep drive. I started noticing a decrease in the amount of time it took me to sleep by the end of my first week. By the third week, I was sleeping more and taking less time to fall asleep consistently. At the end of the program, I was sleeping over 7 hours a night. More importantly, I wasn't spending my days worrying about my sleep.
 CBT-I isn't an easy solution. The sleep schedules are hard to follow and the results aren't instantaneous. It takes time and effort but the reward of permanently improved sleep is well worth it. I'm grateful to have had a coach help me through my journey. Let me know if you do decide to try it and if it helps you improve your sleep. Here's to hoping you get better sleep soon. CBT-I changed my life, but the program cost me $700 out of pocket. I was fortunate to have had the means to afford it but it is well out of the price range for many people. We built Sleepedy with the hopes of making access to a coach-led CBT-I program more affordable. If you'd like to know more, check out our website or download our iOS app below.

在YouTube上推广的所有产品中，哪一个是最好的？虽然我想通过参加一门合法的课程来减少我的学习曲线，但我也不想付钱给那些在亚马逊上实际上并不成功地建立了一项有利可图的业务的人。
亚马逊的商业课程与大多数“如何在线赚钱”课程的规则是一样的：很多都是骗局，但也有一些是合法的学习机会。
所有销售“在亚马逊上建立有利可图的业务”课程的人真的在亚马逊上建立了有利可图的业务吗？绝对不行-在他们开始销售课程之前，没有实体来审查他们的申请。你不一定要自己完成某件事，才能宣称你可以教别人去做。
然而，他们中的一些人做了他们声称能够教的事情。诀窍在于学会如何辨别哪个是哪个。这里有一些避免诈骗的方法。
检查是否符合FTC合规性要求某些索赔的公司必须遵守FTC的商机规则。
这条规则的一个要求是，如果一家公司声称你在购买他们的系统后可以赚到一定数量的钱(“在第一个月赚到1万美元！”)，它必须提供一份收入申报单。这一声明必须提供证据，证明人们确实可以使用他们的方法赚取那么多钱。第二个要求是，该公司必须提供一份关于其业务的披露文件。
如果一家公司是个骗局，它很可能不会遵守联邦贸易委员会的这一规定。在你购买做出这些承诺的课程之前，询问他们的收入申报单和披露文件。如果卖家不能立即提供这些信息，那很可能是个骗局。
然而，阅读细则联邦贸易委员会给骗子留下了一个漏洞。如果课程的卖家声明他们的方法“并不适用于所有人”，他们就可以避免联邦贸易委员会的压力来支持他们的主张。如果你在小字上看到这句话的某些版本，可能意味着很多人在尝试课程倡导的方法时赔钱了。
与以前的学习者联系如果一门课程是合法的，其他人以前就会选修它并取得成功。你可以让卖家帮你联系以前的学员。
当然，这并不能保证他们不会将你与他们付钱购买评论的假“学生”联系起来(或者与冒充学生的卖家联系起来)。然而，如果他们能把你和几个以前的学生联系起来，并且毫不犹豫地这么做，这可能是一个迹象(尽管不能保证)这是一门合法的课程。
如果你确实和以前的学员交谈过，询问他们的具体情况，比如他们实际上能够从他们的业务中赚到多少钱，以及实现这一目标需要花费多少钱。
阅读在线评论总是在你投资一门课程之前在谷歌上快速搜索一下。如果很多人都对它持负面评价或称其为骗局，那就是一个明显的危险信号。然而，请记住，缺乏信息也可能是一个不好的迹象，积极的评论可能是有偿的，也可能是虚假的，所以它们并不是合法性的确切标志。
检查语言骗局往往言过其实，而真正的课程会更切合实际。当然，任何语言都会试图引诱你购买，但寻找太好而不真实的说法是值得的。
诈骗课程经常声称提供了一个“经过验证的系统”，或者保证用最少的工作就能快速获利。真正的课程将现实地看待建立一个有利可图的亚马逊业务所涉及的时间和精力，以及并不是每个尝试过的人都能成功的事实。警惕那些听起来容易的课程。
此外，要警惕那些表明购买课程没有风险的退款政策。诈骗者经常声称他们会退还你的钱，但实际上并不打算这样做。
当心咄咄逼人的免费赠品，许多骗局用免费的电话或咨询把你拉进来。然而，合法的课程也可能试图用免费的东西来吸引你。不同的是，骗子会在谈话中向你施压，要求你立即购买他们的课程。合法的卖家不会试图说服你必须现在买入，以免错失良机。
了解亚马逊的服务条款诈骗者经常出售实际上违反亚马逊服务条款的提示，并可能导致您在网站上被禁止。即使是合法的课程也可能意外地提供违反这些条款的提示。一定要熟悉服务条款，不要把钱花在任何暗示你违反服务条款的课程上。
即使不是为了诈骗你的非诈骗者课程也要警惕，因为它可能不会提供最好的信息。在真正想提供帮助的卖家中，提供的课程质量差异很大。
因此，一旦你确定一门课程不是骗局，就继续批判性地思考，以确定它是否真的值得你花钱。例如，一些课程提供了非常基本的信息，你可以很容易地在其他地方免费获得。
尽管许多课程都声称，在亚马逊上销售没有什么秘密。一门好的课程会简单地整理一些信息，虽然你可以在其他地方找到这些信息，但很难在一个地方找到所有的信息。
亚马逊最好的课程是什么？即使经过仔细的研究，卖家也很难区分哪些课程是合法的，哪些是不合法的。
因为在你选修一门课程(或与卖家和以前的学生详细交谈)之前，很难判断这门课程的合法性，所以我们不能推荐一门具体的课程作为“最好的”。不过，让我们看看您提到的课程，再加上一个可能值得您花费时间和金钱的课程来源。
九大是九大合法吗？快速浏览一下他们的网站就会发现答案可能是否定的。
该网站的许多方面看起来都很有希望，比如主页上详细的成功故事。然而，在页面的底部，有一份免责声明，其中包括这样的文字：“老实说，如果你正在阅读这份免责声明，并想知道这是不是某种‘骗局’，你可能还没有准备好成为一名企业家。”一家合法的公司不太可能对怀疑者如此不屑一顾--相反，它会提供合法性的证据。
该网站也让人很难知道这门课程的费用是多少，但似乎是1,997美元。对于学习如何在亚马逊上获利来说，这是一个非常高的价格：这是许多人自学的。就算九大不是骗局，也可能不值这个价。
丛林童子军有趣的是，丛林童子军实际上并不是一门课程。相反，它是一个订阅网站，帮助亚马逊进行产品研究。你可能最终会决定你不需要它，但这可能不是一个骗局-你可以尝试一下(49美元/月，不是一笔巨大的投资)，然后如果它对你不起作用就取消你的订阅。
电子商务帝国建设者和九大一样，很难说电子商务帝国建设者是不是一个骗局。然而，其网站上的说法听起来肯定好得令人难以置信。
例如，如果您观看免费培训，主页承诺提供“3500美元/周的免费销售漏斗模板”。这是一个很高的要求，而且不太可能每个使用该模板的人都能真正看到这样的销售。
这个网站也让你很难看到费用，但其他消息来源建议你为这门课程支付997美元-对于不能保证的信息来说，这是另一个高昂的价格。记住，即使你找到了一条好的路线，也不能保证你的亚马逊商店一定会成功，因为电子商务世界竞争非常激烈，变化很快。
Udemy如果你真的想参加亚马逊的商业课程，我们建议你从Udemy上提供的课程开始，而不是上面列出的昂贵的大牌课程。
Udemy仅仅是一个聚合各种主题课程的网站。不能保证在这个平台上的课程是有价值的。但好消息是，你可以在那里找到许多低成本的亚马逊FBA课程，这些课程往往有很多用户评论。
参加几个便宜的Udemy课程来学习基础知识，你可能会发现1000美元和2000美元的课程看起来没那么有吸引力。
许多Udemy课程也关注在亚马逊上销售的具体方面，而不是一刀切的“如何盈利”的方法。这让你可以将自己的研究与便宜的Udemy课程结合起来，以填补你的知识空白-如果一门课程最终被证明不是那么好，至少你不会在它上面花费太多钱。
最后想一想，诈骗者很容易使用营销策略来让他们的课程看起来合法。许多备受瞩目的亚马逊课程骗局在买家已经花费了数千美元之前，几乎没有显示出警告信号。
热情洋溢的在线评论和高调的在线出版物中的提及并不能保证一门课程是合法的。需要大量的研究才能弄清楚哪些是骗局，哪些不是，这就是为什么我们不会对这里提到的课程做出任何特定的声明-但我们会说它们看起来太好了，不像是真的。
如果你能轻松地花掉1000美元或更多，并且渴望参加一门昂贵的课程，那很好，但请记住，那里有比合法课程多得多的骗局。即使是合法的也不太可能教会你什么特别革命性的东西。一旦你在一门课程上花了钱，大多数卖家就会使用精明的营销策略来让你继续花钱。因此，我们建议从便宜或免费的信息开始。这可能需要更长一点的时间，但所花费的时间可以为您节省数千美元。

note_en:
Which one is the best of all the ones being promoted on YouTube? While I would like to reduce my learning curve by taking a legitimate course, I also don’t want to pay someone who isn’t actually successful at building a profitable business on Amazon.
       Amazon business courses are subject to the same rule that governs most “how to make money online” courses: many are scams, but some are legitimate learning opportunities.
  Have all the people selling “Build a Profitable Business on Amazon” courses actually built a profitable business on Amazon? Absolutely not - there’s no entity to vet their claims before they start selling courses. You don’t have to accomplish something yourself in order to claim that you can teach someone else to do it.
  However, some of them have done what they claim to be able to teach. The trick lies in learning how to tell which is which. Here are some ways to steer clear of scams.
   Check for FTC Compliance    Companies that make certain claims are required to  comply with the   FTC’s Business Opportunity Rule .
  One requirement of this rule is that, if a company claims you can make a certain amount of money after buying into their system (“Make up to $10,000 in your first month!”), it has to provide an earnings claim statement. That statement must offer proof that people can actually earn that amount using their methods. A second requirement is that the company has to offer a disclosure document about their business.
  If a company is a scam, it probably won’t comply with this FTC rule. Before you buy a course that makes these promises, ask for their earnings claim statement and disclosure document. If the seller can’t provide that information immediately, it’s probably a scam.
   Read the Fine Print   The FTC has   left a loophole  for scammers, though. If the seller of a course states that their method “doesn’t work for everyone,” they can avoid the FTC pressure to back up their claims. If you see some version of this statement in the fine print, it could mean lots of people have lost money trying the methods the course advocates.
   Connect with Former Learners    If a course is legit, other people will have taken it before and succeeded. You can ask the seller to connect you with former students from the course.
  Of course, this doesn’t guarantee that they won’t connect you with bogus “students” who they’ve paid for reviews (or with one of the sellers posing as a student). However, if they can connect you with several former students and don’t hesitate to do so, that may be a sign (though not a guarantee) that it’s a legit course.
  If you do talk to former learners, ask for specifics, such as how much they were actually able to earn from their business and what it took to get there.
   Read Online Reviews   Always do a quick Google search before you invest in a course. If lots of people have reviewed it negatively or called it a scam, that’s an obvious red flag. However, keep in mind that a lack of information may also be a bad sign, and positive reviews can be paid-for or fake, so they aren’t a sure sign of legitimacy.
   Check the Language    Scams tend to over-promise, while genuine courses will be more realistic. Of course, any course will use language that tries to entice you to buy, but it’s worthwhile to look for too-good-to-be-true claims.
  Scam courses often claim to offer a “proven system,” or guarantee fast profits with minimal work. Real courses will be realistic about the time and effort involved in building a profitable Amazon business, and the fact that not everyone who attempts it succeeds. Be wary of courses that make it sound easy.
  Also, be wary of refund policies that suggest there’s no risk involved in buying a course. Scammers often claim they’ll refund your money with no intention of actually doing so.
   Watch out for Pushy Freebies   Many scams rope you in with a free call or consultation. Legitimate courses may also try to hook you with a freebie, though. The difference is that the scammers will put pressure on you to buy their course right away during the conversation. A legitimate seller won’t try to convince you that you must  buy now  to avoid missing out.
   Learn Amazon’s Terms of Service   Scammers often sell tips that actually   violate Amazon’s terms of service  and could get you banned from the site. Even legitimate courses may accidentally offer tips that violate those terms. Make sure to get familiar with the terms of service, and don’t spend money on any course that suggests you violate them.
   Be Wary Even of Non-Scammers   Courses that aren’t aiming to scam you still may not provide the best information. Among sellers who genuinely want to help, there’s a large range in the quality of courses offered.
  So, once you’ve determined a course isn’t a scam, keep thinking critically to determine if it’s really worth your money. Some courses offer very basic information that you can easily get for free elsewhere, for example.
  Despite what many courses claim, there are no secrets to selling on Amazon. A good course will simply collate information that, while you could find it elsewhere, would be hard to find all in one place.
      What’s the Best Amazon Course?    Even with careful research, sellers make it very hard to tell which courses are legit and which aren’t.
  Because it’s so hard to tell the legitimacy of a course until you’ve taken it (or spoken at length with the sellers and former students), we can’t recommend one specific course as the “best.” However, let’s take a look at the courses you mentioned, plus one source for courses that might be worth your time and money.
   Nine University   Is Nine University legit? A quick glance at their website suggests that the answer may be no.
  Many aspects of the site look promising, such as the detailed success stories on the homepage. However, at the bottom of the page, there’s a disclaimer that includes this  text: “Honestly, if you are reading this disclaimer and wondering whether this is a ‘scam’ of some sort, you probably aren’t ready to be an entrepreneur.” A legitimate company isn’t likely to be so dismissive of skeptics - it would offer proof of legitimacy instead.
  The site also makes it difficult to tell how much this course costs, but it appears to be  $1,997. That’s a very high price tag for learning how to profit on Amazon: something that many people have taught themselves. Even if Nine University isn’t a scam, it’s likely not worth that price.
   Jungle Scout    Interestingly, Jungle Scout isn’t actually a course. Instead, it’s a subscription-based site that helps with Amazon product research. You may ultimately decide you don’t need it, but it’s probably not a scam - you can try it (for $49/month, not a huge investment) and then cancel your subscription if it doesn’t work for you.
   Ecommerce Empire Builders   As with Nine University, it’s hard to say for certain whether or not Ecommerce Empire Builders is a scam. However, the claims on its site certainly sound too good to be true.
  For example, the homepage promises a “Free $3,500/Wk Sales Funnel Template” if you watch the free training. That’s a high claim, and it’s very unlikely that everyone who uses the template will actually see sales like that.
  This site also makes it hard to see the cost, but other sources suggest that you’d pay  $997 for the course - another high price tag for information that can’t be guaranteed. Remember that even if you find a good course, there’s no guarantee that your Amazon store will be a success, since the eCommerce world is very competitive and changes fast.
   Udemy   If you do want to take an Amazon business course, we suggest starting with   those offered on Udemy , rather than the high-cost, big-name courses listed above.
  Udemy is simply a site that aggregates courses on a variety of topics. There’s no promise that a course on the platform is valuable. But the good news is that you can find many low-cost Amazon FBA courses there, which often have many user reviews.
  Take a few inexpensive Udemy courses to learn the basics, and you’ll probably find that the $1,000 and $2,000 courses look far less appealing.
  Many Udemy courses also focus on specific aspects of selling on Amazon, rather than a one-size-fits-all “how to make a profit” approach. This lets you combine your own research with cheap Udemy courses to fill in your knowledge gaps - and if a course turns out to be not that great, at least you won’t have spent much on it.
   Final Thoughts    It’s very easy for scammers to use marketing tactics to make their courses appear legitimate. Many high-profile Amazon course scams showed few or no warning signs to buyers until they had already spent thousands.
  Glowing online reviews and mentions in high-profile online publications don’t guarantee a course is legit. It takes a lot of research to figure out which are scams and which aren’t, which is why we won’t make any certain claims about the courses mentioned here - but we will say that they look too good to be true.
  If you can easily spare $1,000 or more and are dying to take a pricey course, that’s fine, but remember that there are far more scams than legitimate courses out there. Even the legitimate ones aren’t likely to teach you anything particularly revolutionary. And once you’ve spent money on a course, most sellers will use savvy marketing tactics to get you to keep spending. So, we recommend starting with inexpensive or free information instead. It may take a little longer, but the time spent could save you thousands of dollars.

title: 关于“在亚马逊上建立有利可图的业务”课程的真相
title_en: The Truth About “Build a Profitable Business on Amazon” Courses

由于不可避免的书呆子气，本文中的Linux指的是标准Linux或GNU/Linux发行版。
人们普遍认为Linux是一个非常安全的操作系统。由于各种不同的原因，这与事实相去甚远。标准桌面中没有强大的沙盒。这意味着所有应用程序都可以访问彼此的数据，并可以窥探您的个人信息。大多数程序都是用内存不安全的语言(如C或C++)编写的，这是大多数已发现的安全漏洞的原因，而诸如控制流完整性等现代利用漏洞缓解措施并未得到广泛使用。内核也非常缺乏安全性。它是一个完整的内核，完全用内存不安全的语言编写，每个月都会发现数百个错误，其中许多是安全漏洞。事实上，在内核中发现了如此多的错误，开发人员无法跟上，这导致许多错误在很长一段时间内没有得到修复。内核在利用缓解方面也落后了几十年，许多内核开发人员根本不关心。在普通桌面上，具有sudo访问权限的受损非root用户帐户几乎等同于完全root危害，因为攻击者有太多方法可以检索sudo密码。通常，标准用户是组sudo的一部分，这使得这是一个巨大的问题，并且使得sudo密码几乎是安全剧场。例如，攻击者可以利用过多的按键记录机会，如X缺少GUI隔离、/proc中的大量信息泄露、使用LD_PRELOAD挂接到每个进程等等。即使我们减轻了记录击键的每一种方式，攻击者也可以设置自己的假sudo程序来窃取用户密码。这就是获取sudo密码所需的全部内容：cat&lt；&lt；\EOF&gt；/tmp/sudo#！/bin/bashif[[&#34；${@}&#34；=&#34；&#34；]]；然后/usr/bin/sudoElse读取-s-r-p&#34；[sudo]${user}：&#34；password echo&#34；${password}&#34；&。\n抱歉，请重试。&#34；/usr/bin/sudo${@}fiEOFchmod+x/tmp/sudoexport path=&#34；/tmp：${path}&#34；
现在攻击者只需一个modbe即可完全访问内核。
标准系统强化不足以解决任何这些大规模的架构安全问题。限制几件小事不会解决这个问题。同样，发行版默认部署的一些常见安全功能也不会解决这个问题。仅仅因为您的发行版在没有创建严格策略的情况下启用了MAC框架，并且仍然不受限制地运行大多数进程，并不意味着您可以摆脱这些限制。一个相当安全的Linux发行版所需的强化程度远远超出人们的想象。您将需要完整的系统MAC策略、完全验证的引导(不仅仅是引导链、整个基本系统)、强大的沙箱体系结构、强化的内核、广泛使用的现代利用漏洞缓解措施等等。实际上正在解决这些问题的一个Linux发行版是Whonix。
Flatpak的目标是沙箱应用程序，但它的沙箱操作存在很大缺陷。它完全信任应用程序，并允许它们指定自己的策略。这意味着安全性是可选的，应用程序可以选择不进行足够的沙箱保护。Flatpak的权限也过于宽泛，没有意义。例如，许多应用程序附带了filessystem=home，这是对用户主目录的读写访问权限，允许访问所有个人文件，并允许通过写入~/.bashrc或类似文件进行简单的转义。Flatpak广泛权限的另一个例子是它如何允许对X11套接字的不经过滤的访问，由于X11&#39；缺乏GUI隔离，因此允许轻松转义。通过Xpra之类的嵌套X11服务器添加X11沙箱很容易，但Flatpak开发人员拒绝承认这一点，并继续声称X11是不可能安全的。更多的例子是Flatpak如何给予目录(如/sys或/proc(以信息泄露而闻名的内核接口)的完全访问权限)。
note_en:
Due to inevitable pedanticism, "Linux" in this article refers to a standard Linux or GNU/Linux distro.
    There is a common assumption that Linux is a very secure operating system. This is very far from the truth for various different reasons.     There is no strong sandboxing in the standard desktop. This means all applications have access to each other’s data and can snoop on your personal information. Most programs are written in memory unsafe languages such as C or C++ which has been the cause of  the majority of discovered security vulnerabilities and modern exploit mitigations such as Control-Flow Integrity are not widely used.     The kernel is also very lacking in security. It is a monolithic kernel written entirely in a memory unsafe language and has hundreds of bugs, many being security vulnerabilities,  found each  month. In fact, there are so many bugs being found in the kernel, developers can’t keep up which results in many of the bugs  staying unfixed for a long time. The kernel is also  decades behind in exploit mitigations and many kernel developers simply  do not care enough.     On ordinary desktops, a compromised non-root user account with access to  sudo is almost equal to full root compromise as there are too many ways for an attacker to retrieve the sudo password. Usually, the standard user is part of group  sudo which makes this a massive issue and makes a sudo password almost security theater. For example, the attacker can exploit the plethora of keylogging opportunities such as  X’s lack of GUI isolation, the  many infoleaks in  /proc, use   LD_PRELOAD to hook into every process and so much more. Even if we mitigate every single way to log keystrokes, the attacker can just setup their own fake sudo program to grab the user password.     This is all it takes to get your  sudo password:  cat <<\EOF > /tmp/sudo#!/bin/bashif [[ "${@}" = "" ]]; then /usr/bin/sudoelse read -s -r -p "[sudo] password for ${USER}: " password echo "${password}" > /tmp/password echo -e "\nSorry, try again." /usr/bin/sudo ${@}fiEOFchmod +x /tmp/sudoexport PATH="/tmp:${PATH}"
   Now the attacker is just a  modprobe away from full kernel access.
    Standard system hardening is not enough to fix any of these massive architectural security issues. Restricting a few minor things is not going to fix this. Likewise, a few common security features distros deploy by default is also not going to fix this. Just because your distro enables a MAC framework without creating a strict policy and still running most processes unconfined, doesn't mean you can escape from these.     The hardening required for a reasonably secure Linux distro is far greater than people assume. You will need full system MAC policies, full verified boot (not just the boot chain, the entire base system), a strong sandboxing architecture, a hardened kernel, widespread use of modern exploit mitigations and plenty more.     One Linux distro that actually is working on fixing these issues is  Whonix.
    Flatpak aims to sandbox applications but its sandboxing is very flawed. It fully trusts the applications and allows them to specify their own policy. This means security is optional and apps can just choose not to be sufficiently sandboxed.     Flatpak's permissions are also far too broad to be meaningful. For example, many applications come with  filesystem=home which is read-write access to the user's home directory, giving access to all of your personal files and allowing trivial escapes via writing to  ~/.bashrc or similar.     Another example of Flatpak's broad permissions is how it allows unfiltered access to the X11 socket, allowing easy escapes due to X11's lack of GUI isolation. Adding X11 sandboxing via a nested X11 server such as Xpra is easy but Flatpak developers refuse to acknowledge this and continue to claim  "X11 is impossible to secure".     Even more examples of this is how Flatpak gives full access to directories such as  /sys or  /proc (kernel interfaces known for information leaks).
title: Linux(In)安全性

note:
(CNN)人工智能是未来。如果“西部世界”或“黑镜”是可信的，那么计算机统治我们所有人的那一天很快就会到来。
美国专利商标局否认了代表人工智能系统DABUS申请的两项专利，并公布了一项裁决，称美国的专利只能授予自然人。
这两项专利分别是食品容器和手电筒，由人工智能研究人员、DABUS&39；创建者斯蒂芬·泰勒(Stephen Thaler)申请。根据美国专利商标局提交的文件，泰勒称DABUS是一台创意机器，并希望人工智能能完全归功于这些发明。文件称，泰勒辩称，允许一台机器被列为发明家将激励使用人工智能系统的创新。CNN已联系泰勒请其置评。
然而，根据USPTO的裁决，发明只能由自然人提交(并根据你想要的哲学程度来构思)，这一点反映在专利法的语言和之前的联邦法院裁决中。
说到哲学，这项裁决引用了1994年联邦巡回法院的一项裁决，该裁决阐述了发明的本质，这种方式肯定会让你的大脑陷入自我反省的迷宫。
构思是发明力的试金石，是完成发明的精神部分。它是发明者头脑中形成的关于完整的、可操作的发明的明确的、永久的想法……。[构思]是一种心理行为.&#34；
在欧洲和英国，将DABUS列为发明者的专利也因与人格有关的类似原因而被拒绝。欧洲专利局还提出了这样一个问题，即在这种情况下，究竟由谁来行使授予发明人的权利。
Thaler是DABUS背后的头脑，是一名物理学家，也是Imagination Engines的创始人，这是一家研究和开发人工神经网络的公司。DABUS就是这样一种人工神经网络。想象力引擎将DABUS描述为一个真正的人工发明家，它被编程为模仿人类思维的神经模式，这些模式导致了神秘的、原始的发明火花。
note_en:
(CNN)Artificial intelligence is the future. If "Westworld" or "Black Mirror" are to be believed, there will soon come a day when the computers rule us all.
  The USPTO  has denied a pair of patents filed on behalf of DABUS, an artificial intelligence system, and published a ruling that says US patents can only be granted to "natural persons."
   The two patents were for a food container and a flashlight, and  were filed by Stephen Thaler, an AI researcher and DABUS' creator. According to the filing from the USPTO, Thaler calls DABUS a "creativity machine" and wanted the AI to get full credit for the inventions. The filing says Thaler argued that "allowing a machine to be listed as an inventor would incentivize innovation using AI systems." CNN has reached out to Thaler for comment.
    However, according to the USPTO's ruling, inventions can only be submitted (and depending on how philosophical you want to get, conceived) by a "natural person," as reflected in the language of patent law and also in previous federal court rulings.
 Speaking of philosophy, the ruling quotes a Federal Circuit court decision from 1994 that expounds on the nature of invention in a way that's certain to send your brain down the maze of reflexive self-awareness.
 "Conception is the touchstone of inventorship, the completion of the mental part of invention. It is the formation in the mind of the inventor, of a definite and permanent idea of the complete and operative invention ... [Conception] is a mental act ..."
 Patents that list DABUS as the inventor  have also been denied in Europe and the UK for similar reasons related to personhood. The European Patent Office also raised the issue of who, exactly, would enforce the rights granted to an inventor under such a circumstance.
   Thaler, the mind behind DABUS, is a physicist and founder of Imagination Engines, a company that researches and develops artificial neural networks.  DABUS is one such artificial neural network. Imagination Engines describes DABUS as a "true artificial inventor" that is programmed to mimic the neural patterns of human thought that lead to the mysterious, primal spark of invention.
title: 专利局表示，人工智能在技术上不能发明东西
title_en: Artificial Intelligence can't technically invent things, says patent office

note:
世界上从来没有见过像Aarogya Setu这样的东西。两个月前，印度还不存在用于追踪冠状病毒接触者的应用程序；现在它已经拥有近1亿用户。印度总理纳伦德拉·莫迪(Narendra Modi)在发布时敦促全国13亿人中的每一个人都下载它，结果在发布后的两周内，它成为有史以来下载量最快的应用程序，下载量达到5000万次。
“我们打败了精灵宝可梦Go，”领导印度政府这项服务开发的阿纳布·库马尔(Arnab Kumar)微笑着说。
但是，尽管这款应用的增长是史无前例的，但它在更重要的方面是非同寻常的：如果你不安装它，你可能会丢掉工作，被罚款，或者坐牢。
根据麻省理工学院技术评论的Covid Tracking Tracker(全球接触者追踪应用数据库)的数据，印度目前是世界上唯一一个强制数百万人使用冠状病毒追踪应用的民主国家。
虽然官方政策是下载这款应用是自愿的，但事实是，政府雇员被要求使用它，而主要的私人雇主和房东也在强制使用它。据报道，诺伊达市现在正在罚款，甚至威胁要逮捕任何没有在手机上安装这款应用的人。
这是戏剧性的一步，引发了全国和全球公民自由专家的猛烈批评。
印度议会知名议员、反对党印度国大党(Indian National Congress)前领导人拉胡尔·甘地(Rahul Gandhi)是批评这款应用的人之一，他指责这款应用“没有机构监督”，并引发了“严重的数据安全和隐私问题”。
“科技可以帮助保护我们的安全，”甘地最近在推特上写道。“但在未经公民同意的情况下，不能利用恐惧来追踪他们。”
数字权利组织Access Now的高级政策分析师埃斯特尔·马塞(Estelle Massé)表示：“这里存在一种侵犯人权的行为，这是没有道理的。”“存在启动一种可在大流行后重新用于监测的工具的风险。”
“麻省理工学院技术评论”的数据库显示，印度的应用程序在许多其他方面也是独一无二的。许多国家正在开发有限的服务，使用蓝牙或GPS向与被发现感染冠状病毒的人进行互动的人发出“暴露通知”。然而，印度的APP是一个庞大的一体化项目，远远超过大多数其他国家正在建设的项目。它跟踪蓝牙联系事件和位置-就像许多其他应用程序一样-但也会给每个用户一个颜色编码的徽章，显示感染风险。除此之外，Aarogya Setu(在印地语中的意思是“通往健康的桥梁”)还提供远程医疗、电子药房和诊断服务。它被所有印度电信公司列入白名单，因此使用它不计入移动数据限制。
这款应用程序的缺失也让它脱颖而出。印度没有国家数据隐私法，也不清楚谁有权访问这款应用程序的数据，在什么情况下也不清楚。在这一点上，访问或使用数据没有强大的、透明的策略或设计限制。开发商名单主要由私营部门志愿者组成，并不完全公开。
库马尔强调，这款应用程序是按照目前正在议会审议的一项数据隐私法案草案的标准建造的，并表示对其收集的数据的访问受到严格控制。但批评人士表达了担忧，因为它不是开源的，尽管印度政府规定其应用程序必须将代码向公众开放。库马尔说，这是Aarogya Setu的一个目标，将在未来发生，但他无法确认时间表或预期日期。
当Aarogya Setu第一次被宣布时，印度政府确实寻求同意，使用这款应用最初听起来像是自愿的。今天，至少有100万人接到了使用它的命令，其中包括中央政府的工作人员和像Zomato和Swiggy这样的私营公司的员工。这在印度是一种实践得很好的策略，在印度，“自愿强制”技术有被用作某些重要权利的把关人的历史。
虽然印度是唯一一个强制数百万人使用其联系人追踪应用的民主国家，但其他民主国家已经与移动电话公司达成协议，可以获取居民的位置数据。在欧洲，这些数据在很大程度上是聚合和匿名的。在以色列，专注于大流行的执法部门使用了一个通常为反恐目的保留的电话跟踪数据库。以色列政府的策略一直是一场法律战的主题，这场官司最终进入了该国的最高法院和立法机构。
这些困难中的许多都可以追溯到缺乏透明度。在发布时，这款应用的隐私政策和服务条款都不是公开的，尽管有要求，但开发人员并没有分享它们。由于这款应用程序不是开源的，其代码和方法不容易被第三方审查，而且没有公开的日落条款说明这款应用程序何时将不再是强制性的，尽管库马尔表示，数据会在患病个人最多60天和健康人最多30天后滚动删除。印度的国家和邦政府将走多远，目前还没有明确的路线图：最近的一篇报道称，政府希望在所有新款智能手机上预装Aarogya Setu；另一篇报道称，这款应用可能很快就会被要求旅行。
在这款应用开发的早期，Kumar表示将利用苹果和谷歌为iPhone和Android联合开发的技术。该系统将在几天后发布，但现在它附带了一些规则，包括要求用户同意和禁止位置跟踪-这两项Aarogya Setu都没有遵守。库马尔说，谷歌工程师一直与Aarogya Setu的开发者保持密切联系，他的团队将评估他们是否仍能实施分散的硅谷系统，该系统旨在保护隐私。谷歌和苹果已经将这款应用快速打入Android和iOS应用商店。
但人们仍然深感担忧的是，模糊自愿和强制之间的界限，以及保护隐私和侵犯隐私之间的界限，将会产生长期的后果。
“国家没有做出任何努力来赢得公民的信任，”公民技术组织Indic Project的执行董事阿尼瓦尔·阿拉温德(Anivar Aravind)说。“这里有一群私营部门的企业志愿者，他们没有任何责任，他们为政府开发了一款应用程序，迫使每个人的个人设备。”
note_en:
The world has never seen anything quite like Aarogya Setu. Two months ago, India’s app for coronavirus contact tracing didn’t exist; now it has nearly 100 million users. Prime Minister Narendra Modi boosted it on release by urging every one of the country’s 1.3 billion people to download it, and the result was that within two weeks of launch it became the fastest app ever to reach 50 million downloads.
  “We beat Pokémon Go,” says a smiling Arnab Kumar, who is leading development of the service for the Indian government.
  But although the app’s growth is unprecedented, it is extraordinary in an even more important way: if you don’t install it, you might lose your job, get fined, or go to jail.
  India is currently the only democratic nation in the world that is making its coronavirus tracking app mandatory for millions of people,   according to MIT Technology Review’s Covid Tracing Tracker, a database of global contact tracing apps.
  While official policy is that downloading the app is voluntary, the truth is that government employees are  required to use it, while major private employers and landlords are  mandating it as well. The city of Noida is now  reportedly fining and even threatening to arrest anyone who fails to install the app on their phone.
  It’s a dramatic step generating fierce criticism from civil liberties experts nationally, and from all over the globe.
  Rahul Gandhi, a prominent member of the Indian parliament and former leader of the opposition Indian National Congress, is among those who have criticized the app, charging that it has “no institutional oversight” and raises “serious data security and privacy concerns.”
  “Technology can help keep us safe,” Gandhi recently tweeted. “But fear must not be leveraged to track citizens without their consent.”
  “There is an infringement on human rights that is not justified here,” says Estelle Massé, a senior policy analyst at the digital rights group Access Now. “There is a risk of initiating a tool that can be repurposed for surveillance after the pandemic.”
    MIT Technology Review’s database shows that India’s app is unique in a number of other ways, too. Many countries are developing limited services that use Bluetooth or GPS to give “exposure notifications” to people who have interacted with someone found to have covid-19. India’s app, though, is a massive all-in-one undertaking that far exceeds what most other countries are building. It tracks Bluetooth contact events and location—as many other apps do—but also gives each user a color-coded badge showing infection risk. And on top of this, Aarogya Setu (which means “a bridge to health” in Hindi) also offers access to telemedicine, an e-pharmacy, and diagnostic services. It’s whitelisted by all Indian telecom companies, so using it does not count against mobile data limits.
  What the app lacks also sets it apart. India has no national data privacy law, and it’s not clear who has access to data from the app and in what situations. There are no strong, transparent policy or design limitations on accessing or using the data at this point. The list of developers, largely made up of private-sector volunteers, is not entirely public.
  Kumar stresses that the app was built to the standards of a draft data privacy bill that is currently in the country’s parliament, and says access to the data it collects is strictly controlled. But critics have expressed concern because it is not open source, despite an Indian government mandate that its apps make their code available to the public. Kumar says that this is a goal for Aarogya Setu and will happen down the line, but he could not confirm a timeline or expected date.
  When Aarogya Setu was first announced, the Indian government did seek consent, and using the app initially sounded voluntary. Today, at least 1 million people have been given orders to use it, including central government workers and employees of private companies like the food delivery services Zomato and Swiggy. It’s a well-practiced tactic in India, where “voluntary mandatory” technology has a history of being used as a gatekeeper to certain important rights.
  While India is the only democracy to make its contact tracing app mandatory for millions of people, other democracies have struck deals with mobile phone companies to access location data from residents. In Europe, the data has largely been aggregated and anonymized. In Israel, law enforcement focused on the pandemic has used a phone tracking database normally reserved for counterterrorism purposes. The Israeli government’s tactics have been the subject of a legal battle that made its way up to the country’s Supreme Court and legislature.
    Many of these difficulties can be traced to a lack of transparency. Neither the privacy policy nor the terms of service for the app were publicly accessible at the time of publication, and the developers have not shared them despite requests. Since the app is not open source, its code and methods can’t easily be reviewed by third parties, and there is no public sunset clause stating when the app will cease to be mandatory, although Kumar says data is deleted on a rolling basis after, at most, 60 days for sick individuals and 30 days for healthy people. And there is no clear road map for how far India’s national and state governments will go: one recent  report said the government wants Aarogya Setu preinstalled on all new smartphones; another said the app may soon be  required to travel.
  In the early days of the app’s development, Kumar said it would leverage the technology being jointly developed by Apple and Google for iPhone and Android. That system will be released in just a few days,  but it now comes with rules that include requiring user consent and banning location tracking—neither of which Aarogya Setu complies with. Kumar says Google engineers have been in close contact with Aarogya Setu’s developers, and his team will evaluate whether they can still implement the decentralized Silicon Valley system, which is intended to preserve privacy. Google and Apple have fast-tracked the app into both the Android and iOS app stores.
  But there are still deep concerns that blurring the line between voluntary and mandatory, and between privacy-preserving and privacy-invading, will have long-term consequences.
  “There is no effort made by the state to earn citizen trust,” says Anivar Aravind, executive director at the civic-technology organization Indic Project. “Here are a set of private-sector corporate volunteers, with no accountability, that built an app for governments that is forced to personal devices of everyone.”
title: 与其他任何民主国家不同，印度正在强迫人们使用其Covid应用程序
title_en: India is forcing people to use its Covid app, unlike any other democracy

note:
前谷歌首席执行官埃里克·施密特认为，在冠状病毒大流行消退后，办公空间的需求将会更大。
施密特周日在“面向全国”节目上发表的言论，似乎与人们普遍认为的一种观点相矛盾，即随着员工学会在家高效工作，办公室可能正在走下坡路。
在做客“面向全国”节目时，这位65岁的老人表示，他认为办公室里社交距离的渴望会让办公空间变得更加昂贵。
施密特说：我猜我们对办公空间的需求会更大，而不是更少，因为人们会想要社交距离。
我们将不得不考虑中心辐射式系统，当地人不会出行这么远，因为他们不想坐这么长时间的公共交通工具。因此，我们将不得不真正重新思考企业是如何运作的。他们需要他们的员工回来。
曾在2001年至2011年领导谷歌的施密特表示，随着同一家公司的员工在不同地点工作，全球特大城市的密度将发生变化。
他说：如果你把它想象成一个雇主，你会有一群员工，他们中的一些人渴望回到办公室，还有一些人担心，如果他们去办公室，他们会死，他说，“如果你把它想象成一个雇主，你会发现他们有一群员工，他们中的一些人渴望回到办公室，还有一些人担心如果他们去办公室，他们会死。”
他们非常担心他们的免疫功能受损，或者你得了什么病。因此，他们将不得不拿出灵活的安排。所以想象一下有三四个人：一个人去办公室，一个人呆在家里，还有一些人去当地或附近的工作环境。它会改变模式。
我们曾经遇到过这样的情况，人们以令人难以置信的集中方式搬到了超级城市。在接下来的几年里，这种情况将会改变。你不需要在这个超级城市里才能参与到这些超级城市的兴奋中来。
CNET周六报道，自2011年以来一直担任谷歌顾问的施密特已于2月份辞去该职位。根据彭博亿万富翁指数，这位前高管仍然是世界上最富有的人之一，净资产为155亿美元。
note_en:
Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt thinks office space will be in greater demand after the coronavirus pandemic subsides.
 Schmidt's remarks on "Face the Nation" on Sunday appeared to contradict a widespread belief that offices might be on the decline as workers learn to work effectively from home.
 During his  appearance on "Face the Nation," the 65-year-old said he thought the desire for social distancing within offices would place a premium on office space.
 "My guess is we'll have more demand for office space, not less, because people will want social distancing," Schmidt said.
   "We're going to have to think about hub-and-spoke systems where local people don't travel so far because they don't want to be in public transit for so long. So we're going to have to really rethink how businesses operate. They need their employees back."
 Schmidt, who led Google from 2001 to 2011, said the density of global megacities would change as employees at the same firm work in different locations.
 "If you think of it as an employer, you have a bunch of employees, some of whom are dying to get back to the office and some people who are afraid that if they go to the office they will die," he said.
 "They're very concerned about they're immunocompromised or what have you. So they're going to have to come up with flexible arrangements. So imagine that there are three or four people: one will go to the office, one will stay home, some will go to some local or near-their-town working environment. It will change the pattern.
   "We've had this situation where people move to supercities in these incredibly concentrated ways. That will change in the next few years. You don't need to be in the supercity in order to participate in the excitement of these supercities."
 CNET reported on Saturday that Schmidt, who's been an adviser to Google since 2011, had stepped down from that position in February. The former exec remains one of the wealthiest people in the world, with a net worth of $15.5 billion,  according to the Bloomberg Billionaires Index.
title: 埃里克·施密特预测，公司在Covid之后将需要更多办公空间，而不是更少
title_en: Eric Schmidt predicts companies will need more office space post-Covid, not less

如果你去掉“办公室”中人物之间的身体互动，并试图通过“松弛”讲同样的笑话，那么“办公室”还能工作吗？
这是一个创意集体MSCHF打算找出的问题，他们在“松弛”上重现了这部电视剧的全部201集。观众将加入现场松弛，不同的频道专门针对不同的部门，包括会计、仓库和一般办公室。请观众避免在“公司”频道发帖，但鼓励观众进出频道以跟上剧集的发展，因为它们并不都在一个频道播放。一位代表告诉The Verge，由于这是一场现场直播，不恰当的信息和图片确实会弹出，但MSCHF团队有一些版主努力让它尽可能地没有巨魔。
有些笑话比其他的更容易翻译；其中一个例子是该剧第二季中的“伤害”。迈克尔·斯科特(史蒂夫·卡雷尔饰)被放在床脚的乔治·福尔曼烤架烫伤了脚，他打电话到办公室寻求帮助。这一集的对话是通过电话进行的，但MSCHF团队使用Slake重新制作了对话，涉及到了原始小品中的所有角色。
MSCHF战略主管丹尼尔·格林伯格(Daniel Greenberg)告诉The Verge：“自从”办公室“播出以来，工作和办公室文化的性质发生了巨大变化，其中很多都围绕着我们使用技术的方式。”“这是真人从周一到周五朝九晚五的现场体验。”
MSCHF在大流行来袭之前就开始了这个项目，但感觉从来没有这么及时过。SLACK和电话会议工具Zoom一样，随着人们离家上班，使用率大幅上升。首席执行官斯图尔特·巴特菲尔德(Stewart Butterfield)几周前在一条冗长的推文中分享了数字；3月10日，Slake的并发用户超过了1000万。到3月25日，它的用户将增加250万。
巴特菲尔德在推特上发帖截图称：“我们的业务发展非常迅速，而且在未来的很多年里还会继续这样做。”他在推特上发的截图显示，“我们的业务令人难以置信，而且这种情况还会持续下去。”“我们提供了一个平台，在未来几年里，这个平台对世界将变得更加有用。”
把“办公室”想象成一部以“松弛”为基础的职场喜剧是很有趣的。就连“办公室”的联合制片人本·西尔弗曼(Ben Silverman)和保罗·利伯斯坦(Paul Lieberstein)也在制作一部新的系列片(另一部工作场所喜剧)，故事完全发生在电脑屏幕上。视觉笑料从闹剧喜剧到GIF，再到公共房间里用斜体写成的“OK Michael”回答。尽管剧本在改编过程中没有改变，格林伯格告诉The Verge，团队确实通过在服务中找到GIF和其他功能来翻译喜剧，从而找到了让笑话在Slake上发挥作用的方法。
每集可能需要大约两到三周的时间才能在“松弛”上“播出”，所以要安顿下来。
note_en:
If you take out  The Office’s physical interactions between characters  and try to tell the same jokes over Slack, does  The Office still work?
 It’s a question that creative collective MSCHF set to find out, recreating all 201 episodes of the series over Slack. Viewers will  join a live Slack, where different channels are dedicated to various departments, including accounting, warehouse, and a general office room. Viewers are requested to avoid posting in the “company” channels, but are encouraged to pop in and out of channels to keep up with the episodes as they don’t all play in one. Since it is a live Slack, inappropriate messages and images do pop up, but the MSCHF team has a number of moderators working to keep it as troll-free as possible, a representative told  The Verge.
 Some jokes are easier to translate than others; one  example used is “The Injury” from the show’s second season. After burning his foot on a George Foreman grill that he keeps at the foot of his bed, Michael Scott (Steve Carell) calls into the office to try to get some help. The conversation takes place over the phone in the episode, but the MSCHF team used Slack to recreate it, involving all the characters from the original sketch.
   “Since  The Office aired, the nature of work and office culture has changed drastically, a lot of which is centered around the way we use technologies,” Daniel Greenberg, head of strategy at MSCHF, told  The Verge. “This is a live experience by real people Mondays through Fridays, nine-to-five.”
 MSCHF started working on the project before the pandemic hit, but it’s never felt so timely. Slack, like teleconferencing tool Zoom, is seeing a massive uptick in usage as people move to work from home. CEO Stewart Butterfield shared numbers in a lengthy tweet thread a few weeks back; on March 10th, Slack’s concurrent users passed 10 million. It would jump by 2.5 million users by March 25th.
 “We have an incredible business that has been growing very quickly and that will continue to do so for many, many years to come,” Butterfield told Slack employees, as seen in  screenshots he tweeted. “We provide a platform that is going to become even more useful to the world in the years to come.”
   It’s fun to imagine  The Office as a Slack-based workplace comedy. Even Ben Silverman and Paul Lieberstein, co-producers on  The Office,  are working on a new series (another workplace comedy) that takes place entirely over computer screens. Visual gags go from slapstick comedy to GIFs and italicized “OK Michael” responses in public rooms. Although the script hasn’t changed in the adaptation process, Greenberg told  The Verge the teams did figure out ways to make the jokes work on Slack by finding GIFs and other features within the service to translate the comedy.
 It could take roughly two to three weeks for each episode to “air” over Slack, so settle in.
title: “办公室”(The Office)201集现在都在“松弛”(Slake)上播出。
title_en: All 201 Episodes of 'The Office' Are Now Playing Out Over Slack

我收集了一些我的照片，并怀着这个目标写下了下面的内容：让你对一个令人惊叹的地方德鲁伊高地(Druid Heights)感到同样的迷恋，就像我在2016年11月16日参观那里之后所做的那样。对于你们所有人，特别是那些在手机上观看的人，一旦你(希望如此)，有70张照片会混入我的描述性文字中。把引言通读一遍。好好享受吧！要了解更多关于这个特殊的地方及其历史的信息，请转到拯救德鲁伊高地Facebook群页。
8/29/18更新：自从这第一个故事以来，我已经在网上发布了几个关于德鲁伊高地的更简短的故事。这是最完整的视图。其他的可以通过使用德鲁伊高地或我的名字Michael Toivonen作为搜索词来搜索Medium来找到。
对我来说，事情是这样开始的：2016年初的一天，一个偶然的机会，我想起了一个被遗忘已久的名字，德鲁伊高地(Druid Heights)。上世纪70年代，我第一次听到这个词是在加州米尔谷(Mill Valley)附近的一所高中，距今已经40年了。除了这个名字，我当时只知道，我一直在读的一位东方宗教作家艾伦·瓦茨(Alan Watts)去世时住在那里，还有一个名叫埃尔莎·吉洛(Elsa Gidlow)的女人，她可能是一位诗人，仍然住在那里。我很感兴趣，但我从未学到更多。
40年后的一个雨天，当这个不同寻常的地名突然出现在我的脑海中时，我准备好了我的笔记本电脑，准备发起一个查询。我发现了一些报纸文章和一些图片。德鲁伊高地似乎比我模糊地想象的要多得多，…。。
在我访问那里的时候，我已经了解到两位主要的创始人，Elsa Gidlow和Roger Somers，都是富有创造力和多产的人，他们的磁性个性非常不同，但也相当互补，特别是在他们共同吸引广泛的重要文化人物到德鲁伊高地的能力方面。尽管德鲁伊高地的面积相对较小，但两位主要创始人的个性差异意味着同时有两个截然不同的社交圈在那里运作，这就增加了它的意义。
出生于英国约克郡和加拿大魁北克省的埃尔莎·吉德洛(Elsa Gidlow，1898-1986)的正规教育在她十几岁的时候就结束了。但到了1954年，当她为5英亩的土地和加利福尼亚州磨坊谷外的一些建筑开出首付支票时。她的天赋、勤奋的工作习惯和大胆使她走得很远。
她靠当记者谋生，并通过诗歌来表达自己，最著名的是她1923年出版的“灰线上”(On A Grey Thread)一书，这是美国作家发表的第一首公开的女同性恋诗歌。她在20世纪20年代初曾在纽约和巴黎生活过，到了那个十年末，她在旧金山站稳了脚跟。在她生命的最后，她的自传“艾尔莎：我带着我的歌来了”成为第一本美国女同性恋者的长篇自传。
在加州，她与当时许多最重要的作家和艺术家成了朋友。她搬进了她来这里叫德鲁伊高地的新家后不久，这些朋友中的许多人，以及来自四面八方的新朋友，都成了常客。
罗杰·萨默斯(1926-2001)是一位富有远见的建设者和工匠，他工作努力，聚会也同样努力或更努力。作为一名有天赋的业余音乐家，他所在的德鲁伊高地很快就成了他结交的许多专业音乐家的圣地。这些人不仅是当地的夜总会球员，他们也是全国和国际知名的音乐家，他们的名字至今仍然家喻户晓。罗杰还有很多朋友都是艺术家和作家，他们也来拜访和聚会。
罗杰·萨默斯负责设计和建造德鲁伊高地最具特色的建筑，并将一些以前的业主改建成如此不同的建筑，以至于在某些情况下几乎认不出来。1965年，他作为一名建筑工人的能力吸引了年轻的埃德·斯泰尔斯来到德鲁伊高地。多年来，艾德，一位才华横溢的家具制造商和建筑商，也为德鲁伊高地的建筑做出了许多重要贡献。作为一个自己做过一些建筑和木工工作的人，罗杰和埃德在德鲁伊高地的工作，一旦我了解到，最初是最吸引人的。
在对德鲁伊高地产生兴趣几个月后，我联系了我高中时代的一位老朋友，我认为他可能知道怎么去那里，这是正确的。在秋天一个阳光明媚的工作日早晨，我徒步旅行，到达了德鲁伊高地。我在那里花了几个小时尽我所能地检查空置的建筑物，幸运的是我拍了很多照片。然而，令我惊讶的是，我发现空荡荡的建筑没有锁上，有些建筑的状况非常糟糕，进入里面似乎不安全。尽管如此，我还是发现了足够多的东西来观看和拍照。
除了破旧的建筑，德鲁伊高地的一部分过去是，现在仍然居住着那些在他们曾经拥有的房屋和土地上有生命租约的人。他们的区域贴着“禁止擅自闯入”的牌子，我离他们远远的。
在3个月后的一次回访中，我计划拍更多的照片。但我原本希望做的事情已经不再可能，因为所有空置的建筑现在都贴上了国家公园管理局(National Park Service)的“危险：请勿进入”的牌子。国家公园管理局是拥有整个德鲁伊高地的政府机构。
在我第一次访问后的几个月里，我继续尽可能多地了解德鲁伊高地。我学得越多，就越觉得德鲁伊高地应该作为一个独特的国家历史遗迹保存下来。自20世纪50年代中期以来，德鲁伊高地独特的建筑庇护了如此多的作家、艺术家、音乐家和精神思想家。在我一生中参观了许多历史遗迹后，我开始相信德鲁伊高地的遗址和故事非常值得加入他们的行列，如果它丢失了，损失将是悲惨的。
由于所有的建筑现在都是禁区，我决定分享一下我在秋天拍摄的照片中发现的东西，并附上一些说明文字。一年多后，我现在对历史有了更多的了解，但我会保持简短的描述，让图片来说话。我希望在看完我在那次参观中看到的东西后，你也会觉得德鲁伊高地是一个值得拯救的历史遗迹！
下面是一张6英亩德鲁伊高地遗址的粗略地图，这是我和其他人在2017年夏天成立拯救德鲁伊高地小组后发给我的。它将帮助您了解总体布局。原版对德鲁伊高地的位置有一些线索，但它们已经被熄灭了。
我是其中之一的拯救德鲁伊高地组织不支持未经授权访问德鲁伊高地，我们也不会给出方向。在这个时候，去那里是不利的，而不是为了保护。成为保护的支持者，加入拯救德鲁伊高地Facebook小组，了解更多信息，并及时了解拯救这个独特地方的最新努力。
我参观德鲁伊高地的日期是2016年11月16日。一次偶然的机会，我选择了德鲁伊高地历史上一个重要的日子到来：艾伦·瓦茨逝世43周年纪念日(1915-1973)。
我当时没有地图，对布局也不太了解。有人告诉我，有些地区仍然有人居住，我应该避开那些地区。徒步到达时，我遇到了另外两个人，一男一女，他们在艾伦去世周年纪念日在他的图书馆举行了一场仪式冥想。我原本打算先去那里，但在得知他们的计划后，我决定把它作为我的最后一站。我告诉他们我的意图，他们感谢我。
他们说我应该沿着这条路走下去，一定不要错过他们所说的“龙屋”，后来我才知道它也为其他人所知，包括对它今天的样子负有最大责任的人，已故的罗杰·萨默斯(Roger Somers)，也就是双峰楼。在上面的地图上，它被称为牧场之家，因为它是该房产早期作为小宅基地或农场的原始建筑。
离开图书馆区域，我走向我认为我会找到德鲁伊高地联合创始人埃尔萨·吉洛(Elsa Gidlow)的房子。有人告诉我龙屋就在它的正对面。
我沿着土路走去，去艾尔莎家的路出现在我的左边。因为它的陡峭，车道是铺设的，这是我在德鲁伊高地唯一看到沥青的地方。
当我走到车道顶端时，我看到右手边有一座小楼。在地图上，这是一个小的红色正方形，靠近Gidlow House的红色矩形。在德鲁伊高地的政府报告中，它被称为冥想小屋，但我被告知它也被称为女神小屋。如上所述，德鲁伊高地的几座建筑不止一个名字。
我从来没有见过瓦片边缘切成这样的图案，所以我不得不仔细看一看。
走到另一边，我发现了这扇漂亮的小平面窗户，可以直接看到另一边的景色。
离开冥想小屋，我朝前面左手边的一座小房子走去。起初很难真正了解它，因为周围都是灌木丛和树木。我很确定那一定是艾尔莎·吉德洛的房子。
它显然比我早些时候透过灌木丛和冥想小屋看到的图书馆更古老。后来我了解到，这是1943年为这处房产的前所有者阿尔方斯和塞尔玛·哈帕的女儿建造的。它只有大约800平方英尺，所以很多人可能会称它为小屋，而不是房子。但是，虽然它的体积小，设计简单，但它似乎建造得很好，而且是建立在完全混凝土的周边基础上的。
下一步，我向西驶向那一边可能腐烂的无法修理的甲板。隐藏在它附近的树叶和杂草下面的是一个混凝土庭院。
在房子的另一端，有证据表明千钧一发：一棵树倒下了，差一点就撞到了几英尺。
在那棵树刚刚错过吉洛屋的左边是一扇向卧室望去的大观景窗。在这里，我第一次看到了非常传统的室内设计。
现在我朝房子的前门走去。我不得不说，当我发现它没有锁的时候，我相当震惊。既然如此，没有任何迹象表明我不能这么做，我就进去了。
一进门，我就向左转，发现自己在厨房里。水槽似乎是旧的一体式预制件的一部分。当我拍这张照片的时候，我身后是一些橱柜，后来我得知是罗杰在埃尔莎搬进来后不久建造的。
对于这么小的房子来说，起居室相当宽敞。壁炉做工精良，看起来像是1943年房子建成时的样子。左边现在空着的书架似乎是后来增加的，我后来才知道是这样。和厨房橱柜一样，埃尔莎搬进来后不久，罗杰就建造了这些橱柜，用来存放埃尔莎的藏书。我参观的那天，嵌入式沙发上有几本书，但它们看起来像是最近来的客人留下的。
现在是时候找到图书馆里的人所说的“龙屋”了，或者是地图上所说的“牧场之家”。穿过树丛中的灌木丛，我看到了前方高耸在我头顶上的一个非常不同寻常的屋顶的轮廓。
当我走近时，我看到了一扇相当奇怪的窗户。玻璃是方形的，但就在它的后面似乎是一堵有一个大圆孔的墙。从圆形开口的侧面伸出的是薄板，似乎形成了两个架子。
沿着双峰、龙屋或牧场屋的西侧漫步(随便你挑！)。在寻找一条入口时，我发现自己走在一条砖石小路上，上面有一些非常不寻常的设计。
如果你看着悲伤的腐烂甲板的左侧，你可以认出上一张照片中显示的路径。后来我了解到，这里看到的较低的“矛”支撑着一种紫藤藤，直到几年前。如果你放大这张照片，就可以看到支撑它们的链条一直延伸到墙上。幸运的是，这座房子的屋顶悬挑相当宽，只要屋顶完好无损，这一特征确实有助于保护建筑免受水破坏。
由于艾尔莎·吉德洛的房子已经开放，双子峰似乎也有可能开放。在检查之前，我向东走去，以便更好地看一看。从另一边看这是不可能的，因为有这么多的灌木丛。除了能更好地看到独特的屋顶线外，我还注意到在屋顶上使用不同颜色的沥青瓦片制作了一个非常不寻常的图案。在我59年的时间里，无论是在书上还是在网上，我都看过很多不同寻常的房子，但我记得从来没有见过像双峰屋顶这样的房子。
这是一个特写镜头。我很高兴我刚买了一台像样的新尼康相机！我并不认为自己是一个很好的摄影师，但这有助于弥补我的缺点。
现在我回到了双峰酒店。很明显，我不应该试图接近那些似乎进入起居室的门，因为我需要走过腐烂的甲板才能做到这一点。东侧看起来好像还有其他的门。为了接近他们，我发现自己走在另一段非常不寻常的小路上。
现在在我的左边有一扇不同寻常的窗户，看起来厨房就在里面。这是不寻常的，因为屋顶的支撑物在哪里？拐角处不是应该有一根柱子吗？屋顶是如何支撑的仍是个谜…。
沿着这条小路再走下去，我正朝你上面看到的方向走去。我不得不说，在这一点上，我的下巴感觉它像是挂在相当低的…。..多好的地方啊，我还没进去呢！
我不确定我前面看到的是什么，但看起来右边是一扇半开的门，门上有一个凹陷的黄色面板，形状像一把吉他。在走向它的路上，左边看到的开口似乎是进入了一个储藏区，但考虑到细节的注意，它肯定不是用来放置普通物体……的。我走过去，把门完全打开。
这就是我所看到的景色。我走进浴室，第一次瞥见了德鲁伊高地联合创始人罗杰·萨默斯(Roger Somers)标志性的室内橱柜。请注意，中间的凹室有一面枢转镜。后来，我从他的儿子泰戈尔那里了解到，双峰别墅的大部分改建工作在1962年左右完成。
如果你想知道为什么里面这么亮，这里的答案是：一个非常大的天窗。
当我再往里走的时候，我发现了水槽和马桶，还有更多建在储藏室里的东西。
Medium.com似乎无法处理垂直纵横比照片。或者至少我不能让它们工作。在大多数情况下，将它们修剪到接近正方形是没有问题的。但是在这里它不起作用，因为会损失太多，所以我把它一分为二。我拍了这张照片，上一张照片中的水槽和厕所在我的右边，回头看着我进来的门，也在房间的另一端的右边。不要错过远端的瓷砖淋浴，凹陷的天花板灯板，然后是两幅图像中较低部分前景中地板上的木板。
我低头看着地板上的两块木板，看到了铰链。很好奇，我弯下腰，掀开了边缘。在木板下面，我发现了双峰住宅浴室的另一个令人惊讶的特点：地板上嵌着一个老式的浴缸。
我应该用闪光灯的，但直到太晚了，才意识到这一点，意思是回家。马桶就在左边，上面照片中可以看到的抽屉显示马桶和水槽在右边，我们正在看厨房。请注意石板地板上的图案与小路外面的图案非常相似。双峰大厦一半以上的楼层都是石板。
往前走，我发现自己站在厨房窗户的另一边。天花板看起来很低，的确如此。在柜台之间的中心位置较高。天花板上似乎全是隐藏在玻璃纤维板后面的灯具。后来我被告知是这样的。
我准备离开厨房，我转过身去，透过一个有一扇凹进的滑门的开口，向看起来像是客厅的角落里有一个柴炉。
离开厨房向右转，我从房间中央看到了这幅景色。后来我了解到，垮掉的一代诗人加里·斯奈德(Gary Snyder)在50年代末帮助罗杰将1926年简单的哈帕“牧场房子”改建成你在这里看到的样子，然后在60年代末在里面住了一年多。而这只是这座房子里倒下的历史的“冰山一角”。下沉桌外面的推拉门可以把它与远处的房间隔开。仔细看看右边，你会看到一条很窄的走廊通向一个小后面的房间。不幸的是，我没有拍下那个房间的照片。房间很朴素，但通向它的大厅不是…。。。
与此同时，我转身向左转。还记得我们从厨房看到的那个柴炉吗？就在它的左边，墙上安装着一块竖琴般的音板。它可以弹奏，也可以弹奏。或者通过扔碎石来引起共鸣，罗杰·萨默斯的儿子泰戈尔说，他的父亲在聚会中做了几次，以引起人们的注意。
这就是那条走廊。我不得不把这张照片夹起来，让它合身，我必须躺在地板上才能拍到。右边是一幅手绘壁画，展示了德鲁伊高地的桉树，天花板上更多的是罗杰定制的凹灯。
检查完那个狭窄的大厅和尽头的房间后，我回到起居室，穿过敞开的书架门，进入了一个完全用书架镶板的房间。为了让它们更耐用，罗杰用玻璃纤维板代替了宣纸。现在我正从外面看清楚我早先看到的那扇圆窗。
这扇窗户里面和周围的木制品很简单，但效果简直令人惊叹。
从圆窗往外看，我转到左边，看到墙上的壁纸被推到一边，露出黑暗角落里的一个壁橱。里面有东西，但我没想到要带手电筒。我确实有相机闪光灯，所以我拍了一张照片，这就是照片显示的情况。还记得我们见过的所有凹进的灯吗？还有很多是我们没有的。
后来有人告诉我，在50年代末或60年代初，你不能开车去家得宝(Home Depot)，或者在米尔谷古德曼(Mill Valley Goodman)的建筑供应部(Building Supply)买一盒调光开关，如果这是你想要的效果的话。罗杰要么是自己建造的，要么是自己建造的，这是所有双峰住宅灯光的定制控制面板。
现在我准备离开双峰别墅了。嗯，实际上我真的不是，但是我觉得我应该这样做。接下来的几张照片是在双峰或牧场之家和上面地图上所谓的费耶之家之间的空地上拍摄的。我会尽我所能地添加描述，但我过去和现在对接下来的几张图片中的内容的了解仍然非常有限。
仍然有景观的残留物，比如这些像蝴蝶花一样的花。几种植物完全反季节开花，好像是春天。后来我被告知，这在经历了大量夏季雾的地区并不少见。秋天来了，下了一场小雨，随着云量的整体减少，他们认为是春天了。
一辆旧的，可能是20世纪90年代的奥迪轿车，贴着2005年的贴纸。后来我了解到，上一次有人住在现在空置的建筑里是在2006年左右。我猜这个被遗弃了…。。。
在离奥迪轿车不远的地方，有一个地方看起来曾经是一个用栅栏围起来的花园，我猜那里有蔬菜。
